BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Break-even check prepared for Crestline client Iron Branch Martial Arts (owner Aaron Delgado). Prompted by Aaron flagging that a property-tax pass-through may bump the studio lease. Pre-formal review with the client.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Andrea to revisit with the client once Iron Branch's 2026 actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 -> 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Aaron)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Aaron said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~28-30 members at the current cost structure. At 55 members the studio has plenty of headroom. Main concern is the seasonal dip, but even at 48 (December low) Iron Branch stays profitable.
ActionItems: Wait for Aaron to confirm the property-tax pass-through before finalizing the lease scenario.
Revisit the model with the client once 2026 actuals close.
